Very Large Coin Purse  
 

Lore Item
WT: 0.1 Weight Reduction: 70%
Capacity: 12 Size Capacity: LARGE
Very Large Coin Purse
Lore Item
WT: 0.1 Weight Reduction: 70%
Capacity: 12 Size Capacity: LARGE

Item Type:Container
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:70 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:70
Submitted By:zatoborge
Lucy Entry By:lowkey
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-20 03:45:02
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Average Price: 35,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 58
[Drops | Quests | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Skyshrine
NPC Name
Lord Yelinak

Quests

This item is used in quests.
Expansion List - Premium only.

Crescent Reach (Removed)
Quest Name
Container Tokens

The Bazaar (Removed)
Quest Name
Container Tokens

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Skyshrine
Reduces weight of the coins you are carrying.


Uploaded September 4th, 2017 by Nniki
Send a correction

I still see people attempting to sell this for excessive amounts in the bazaar, but its actual function is now useless since sony reduced all coin weight to zero so people has platinum on them for mercenary costs.

387 posts

# May 20 2005 at 9:35 PM Rating: Default
iganias
210 posts

▲ Page top
Monk item? > EVERYONES ITEM.
QuoteReply
# Dec 29 2003 at 7:29 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1618186616419
146 posts
I"m a monk and would love this bag.
But, its really suited to anybody.
Juast about anybody OTHER than a monks will be the ML, so truthfully, its good for anyone.

Whats my take on it? If I get 1000pp in my pocket before I get to the bank, its pretty damn rare.
Only happended once. I generally do at most 2 LDON adv before the party breaks up anyway.
Ya, monks get less AC from weight, but a few hundred plat isn't gonna kill me.

In fact, unless the tank somehow bites the dust, or I"m taking hellovalot of AE dmg from a mob, I rarely get hit anyway. Maybe once or twice in an average LDON adv the cleric has to give me a CH. most of the time I just get a HoT between fights.



Edited, Nov 6th 2006 at 4:10pm PST by jjamesge
▲ Page top

Becomes no drop if you have a no drop item inside..just like any other bag. Won one tonight, transferred it to my alt.

QuoteReply
# Sep 14 2003 at 10:34 PM Rating: Good

19 posts
It is not a no-drop item.

I was just awarded one today, and it was not awarded until we had left the zone and moved on to other areas. It certainly is nice though. :)
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 06 2002 at 3:00 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Where is the damn affordable item that reduces the coins you carry 100% of the weight. I am really very sick of having to keep my weight down to nothing, I want to loot coin by the barrel full no matter if i'm a lvl 1 newbie or a lvl 65 uber guy with all the best armor in the game. Ah well, I wouldn't be complaining if they hadn't nerfed our AC defense so they deserve every rant they get after that.
RE: Sigh
QuoteReply
# Dec 10 2002 at 5:44 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592247435153
497 posts
If you wanted a character with good AC and dmg, perhaps you should not have chosen a monk.... just throwing that out there. Even pre-nerf they weren't supposed to be tanks. Post-nerf they still aren't, but put them in the fight with a good MT, and they're invaluable (along with rogues).

Are you just upset because you can't solo like you used to?
▲ Page top
coins
QuoteReply
# May 10 2002 at 5:55 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966030614
View User Forum
20 posts
Am a warrior & would love this item as I can not just tp to the nearest bank to unload my cash. This would turn 3 trips to the bank into 1. Seem to spend half my time tryin to get a tp to a bank to unload my cash & the other half tryin to get back. Any characters could use this. Agreed a monk probably most, but I think all are entitled.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 06 2002 at 10:41 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Monkeys get this and dragon bags, thats just how it is. If you don't like it play a monk. If all the monks have, casters gonna get. If you don't like it, play a caster. Melee could give two ***** about this bag, we got 255 str all the time, and WE are the ones that KEEL big stuff.

EQ boards are just silly.
QuoteReply
# Jan 22 2002 at 2:53 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592247318635
View User Forum
8 posts
This place is getting as bad as Everlore. People just flame each other and provide no useful info that someone can actually use about the item in question, other than reiterating the info already available in the picture. I am pretty sure that ANY class would love a 70% coin weight reducer. So quit flaming monks or anyone else that says "gee I would really like this for <insert class here>". And yes I am a monk, but that makes no difference to the point.
▲ Page top
RE: EQ boards are just silly.
QuoteReply
# May 06 2003 at 3:22 PM Rating: Excellent
Illia
View User Forum
Guru
2,553 posts
Or just use the content controls in the forum system here. Setting the system to only display Good or better comments removes the bickering pretty well. You'll miss the odd fresh commment with good content, but if you're that opposed to watching people bicker it's probably worth it.

Use the Preferences to set the system to "Side View, Always Expand, Newest first, Good and better", select save, and click change. Can't really complain about the comments when I've built a whole system so that people can ignore them at their own liking.
____________________________
--Illia
Fumus, draco magus incoluit mare.
Myrx - 70 Holy Priest, Myr - 70 Resto Shaman, Gryd - 70 Prot Warrior

kinda ironic, eh?

blast this gnome logic!

"it was broken when I found it, honest!"
▲ Page top
RE: Funny...
QuoteReply
# Dec 22 2003 at 4:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1690762641916
81 posts
Quote:
how when you think about it, if you're carrying around 38K of plat, you would need this purse. BUT since you're buying it, the guy or gal who just sold it to you would seem to need it more!
kinda ironic, eh?


This would be why, prior to the weight restrictionless Bazaar, most trades of this nature were performed at a bank, and now are performed at a bank or in the Bazaar. That way no one has to travel a significant distance carrying a massive amount of cash that slows them down.

41 posts
that is so true. wow when u think of it the person who just sold it would be liek "sweet 38k........ i can't move to get to bank..oo poo nuggets..." hah i wish i could see that
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 01 2002 at 1:14 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) internet prices = ****
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 06 2001 at 9:13 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I don't know where all the people saying 20% got that from, the pic and description say 70%!!!
monk BS
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2001 at 4:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762117177
2 posts
If I remember correctly, the reason monks have the weight limits and are decently equipped in player made armor is because verant wanted a class for those who didn't want to spend every gaming moment looking for loot. So, complaining about the lack of "monk gear" is like complaining about the lack of warrior spells, or good mage melee items. True, it is still possible to upgrade any monk's gear, but it wouldn't be quite fair to make a class that will never need gear. As it is, it is much cheaper to buy a full set of high level monk armor and weapons than it is for any other melee class, and if you really want, you are the only class who at high levels can be effective without having spent any money on weapons or spells.

I play a monk. I accepted the weight restrictions, limited armor and other drawbacks when I started him. If your real problem is never getting to loot, you're playing the wrong class. Rolling when it's actually a decent monk usable item I agree on, but rolling just to roll is stupid. And if you really want monk gear, go to the zones that have it, don't just go to any zone then start demanding for stuff to drop gear for you.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 12 2004 at 7:26 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) You know that would make sense except for the fact that monk resists and stats do not go up without gear. If my svm was at 120 at level 50 with tailored gear only, i'd be very happy.
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 07 2002 at 10:08 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) The big difference is when your monk is your main. At level 36/37 I am JUST starting to get money to buy stuff, but it's commin in real slow. Maybe when I hit 50 I might be able to upgrade me equipment.
RE: monk BS
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2001 at 11:28 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592756849311
14 posts
"If I remember correctly, the reason monks have the weight limits and are decently equipped in player made armor is because verant wanted a class for those who didn't want to spend every gaming moment looking for loot."

You remember wrong. The whole idea for a monk is to be able to do dmg and avoid being hit.

All the coin purses reduce coin weight..

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 09 2001 at 1:35 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is a great item for a druid twinking a monk.
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 09 2001 at 1:35 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is a great item for a druid twinking a monk.
Great Item
QuoteReply
# Jun 09 2001 at 2:28 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757381256
26 posts
The largest benefit I see in this item, is high levels on old servers. And yes, this is most definitely a great item for Monks. The thing is, on Tarrew mArr for instance.. I know a LOT of people that have tons of PP in bank that they want on hand for some item when it drops or a spell. But, that is just not possible since THAT much PP weighs a ton. And I agree with one of the earlier posts.. Kudos for adressing the earlier problem of too few slots in the Coin Bags, I presonally have never had one, but "if" these Bags also reduce Item weight.. Then they're just THAT much better.
-Horaxe Dreamwalker 54 Shaman, Tarrew Marr
▲ Page top
RE: Great Item
QuoteReply
# Sep 29 2001 at 7:52 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Carry around a stack of some valuable gem. .1 weight, stacks up to 20 in one slot. weight problem solved. Most other players will know approximate value of the gems, and will trade accordingly. Needless to say, buy from friendly vendor with charisma as high as you can.
▲ Page top
RE: Great Item
QuoteReply
# May 06 2003 at 1:47 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592255076925
View User Forum
172 posts
the only trouble is you lose about 10% when you buy from a vendor and sell back at best prices....

if you don't want to lose 10%
you can get up to 100 in JC, carry around a jewelers kit, a stack of enchanted silver bars and a stack of rubies.
each ruby costs 131.25 plat, each gold bar costs .45 plat.
click combine, sell to vendor for 137.45 plat.
1 in 20 will fail, so a stack should yield 2611.25 plat, and cost 2635.5 plat, for a loss of 1 percent. the weight of enc. silver bars is .3, rubies .1, jewelers kit .4

hey, it may be a waste of time and crazy, but it works if you're a complete penny pincher.
▲ Page top
im poor
QuoteReply
# Jun 08 2001 at 8:28 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591761834802
4 posts
im so poor i dont hafta' worry about the weight my coins. is this good or bad?...

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 08 2001 at 4:29 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I could be wrong, after all, I don't have velious, or kunark, so there are a lot of things and places I've never seen, but it doesn't say 'effect' on it, so I don't think it affects the weight of the coins you are carrying, just the items stored within it. If it does indeed alter the weight of the coin you are carrying, then Verant has made another typo...
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 12 2001 at 1:12 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If you dont have either of the expansions then you really shouldn't be trying to provide comments or info on things in them.

My thought is that think about how close this is to 100% coin weight reduction? Now think of the fact that with 100% weight reduction you will never have to worry about how much coin you have again. So with this, you can NEARLY not think about coin at ALL.

# Jun 08 2001 at 9:26 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Well, as a iksar monk I'd love this. Running through cities and doing a FD at the bankers feet gets a bit tedious after a while. Since coins weigh 1.0 per 40 coins, this'd really cut the number of trips through hostile cities.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 11 2001 at 6:47 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ha ha ha i never really tried that but it might work except half the time FD doesnt work
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 13 2001 at 9:18 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) then practice fd
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 08 2001 at 5:58 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Although the idea sounds good, in theory these don't really do all that much. With the other coin purse that drops from mobs in skyshrine, it simply doesn't help that much. You have to be carrying around an insane amount of coin before it really would earn it's keep in your inventory.
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 08 2001 at 9:09 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is meant to reduce the weight of COIN. No other item in the game even comes close. The only other one reduces 20%. If you have to watch weight, this makes a HUGE difference.
